title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI is changing the software building process and the risks of black-box abstraction. The talk emphasizes staying close to material implementation details and preserving craft rather than losing understanding through opaque AI layers. Key concern is whether AI tooling maintains human connection to the underlying system.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Ryo Lu of Cursor examines how AI-assisted development is reshaping the software building process and warns against the risks of black-box abstraction. The argument is that opaque AI layers can erode a builder's understanding of the underlying system, so the priority should be staying close to material implementation details and preserving craft. The central question raised is whether AI tooling maintains a human connection to the system rather than hiding it behind abstraction.
